steps.title: Identify what the finding suggests about lobbying
steps.content: The passage states lobbying does little to alter the probability of policies being enacted. This finding portrays lobbying as lacking effect or impact.

steps.title: Match the word to the meaning
steps.content:
The blank fills "lobbying is not as ______ as this finding seems to suggest" -- meaning the word must describe what the finding implies about lobbying (i.e., something negative), while the sentence as a whole pushes back because lobbying does shape which proposals come forward. Substitute each word:

A. Counterproductive means actively causing harm rather than helping. The finding shows lobbying has little effect on enactment -- not that it backfires. This overstates the criticism beyond what the evidence supports.

B. Cynical means distrustful or believing people act from selfish motives. The finding is about lobbying's effectiveness, not anyone's motives -- this doesn't fit the context.

C. Biased means favoring one side unfairly. The finding is about lobbying's impact on probability, not about fairness or objectivity -- this doesn't apply.

D. Ineffectual means failing to produce the desired result. The finding that lobbying barely changes enactment probability implies it is ineffectual -- but Kang notes lobbying still shapes which proposals are brought forward, proving it is not as ineffectual as that finding alone suggests. ✓

D is correct.
